response:
during the cold war the united states was led by several presidents including truman kennedy nixon and reagan while the soviet union was led by leaders such as stalin khrushchev brezhnev and gorbachev the cold war was a period of political and military tension between the two superpowers with no direct large scale warfare but many proxy conflicts and covert operations the leadership of each country played a crucial role in shaping the course of events during this time the us focused on containing the spread of communism while the soviet union aimed to expand its influence globally the cold war ended with the collapse of the soviet union in 1991 marking the end of a long period of global tension and rivalry
